qa_pairs.question: How do you run an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) mode using a Slurm script on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To run an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in SMP mode, you would use the `mapdl` command with the `-smp` flag, for example: `mapdl -smp -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`. This command also sets an output directory named `outdir-$SLURM_JOBID` and uses your specified input file.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are required to be loaded in a Slurm script to run Ansys Mechanical APDL?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03` (or a newer version of the Ansys module) in your Slurm script.

qa_pairs.question: What specific environment variable configuration is necessary when running Ansys Mechanical APDL on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: When running on the Cedar cluster, you must include `[[ "$CC_CLUSTER" = cedar ]] && export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$EBROOTGCC/../lib/gcc` in your Slurm script to correctly set the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`.
